channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: PSG beats Brest 3-0 in Champions League ahead of Man City and Real Madrid clash
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Paris Saint-Germain took a big step toward the Champions League round of 16 by beating Brest 3-0 in a first-leg playoff match Tuesday. Ousmane Dembélé scored twice and Vitinha converted a penalty to put the French champion in control.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA All-Star Game salutes Bay Area music scene with great lineup of talent
item.description: E-40, En Vogue, Saweetie among Bay Area stars set to appear at NBA All-Star Game festivities at Golden State Warriors' Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrington, The Mercury News

item.title: Raiders, Cowboys and Dolphins go another season long removed from their championship days
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — The sayings “commitment to excellence” and “just win, baby” encapsulated what the Raiders represented as they bulldozed their way to three Super Bowl titles with a take-no-prisoners style that endeared and villainized them.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Guy Gaudreau joins US team for practice at the 4 Nations Face-Off
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — Guy Gaudreau spoke softly, cracked a joke about how bad a hockey player he was and nodded his head with appreciation. On the lapel of his jacket was a pin honoring the memory of his sons, John and Matt.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles' Super Bowl-winning coordinator Kellen Moore will become the Saints head coach
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Kellen Moore, who oversaw the Super Bowl champion Philadelphia Eagles ' offense this season, has agreed to return to the site of his latest triumph as the next coach of the New Orleans Saints.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Champions League global rights deal set to be struck by U.S. agency Relevent with UEFA, clubs
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — UEFA and top European soccer clubs are set to drop the Swiss agency that has sold Champions League rights since 1992 and replace it with the U.S. firm of Miami Dolphins owner Stephen Ross.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sidney Crosby says he's good to go for Canada at 4 Nations after missing Penguins games with injury
item.description: BROSSARD, Quebec (AP) — After missing Pittsburgh's final two games before the NHL break for the 4 Nations Face-Off, Sidney Crosby took part in Canada's first practice Monday and declared himself good to go.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 Nations Face-Off teams 'jump right in' with little time to spare before the tournament starts
item.description: BROSSARD, Quebec (AP) — The last time Jon Cooper coached an international tournament featuring the NHL's best players, the 2016 World Cup of Hockey came with a training camp and a slate of exhibition games to get ready. The 4 Nations Face-Off has none of that.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles captain Lane Johnson says it will be 'team decision' to go to White House if Trump extends invite
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles have won Super Bowl LIX, which begs the question: Will President Donald Trump extend an invitation to the White House after what happened in 2018?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles lineman wins 3rd consecutive Super Bowl title
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles offensive lineman Darian Kinnard won a third consecutive Super Bowl title on Sunday as his team defeated the Kansas City Chiefs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Swiss skier Franjo von Allmen wins downhill gold at world championships
item.description: Franjo von Allmen won gold in the downhill at the Alpine skiing world championships on Sunday as the Swiss team swept the men's speed events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canada beats US 3-1 in Game 5 to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase
item.description: Jennifer Gardiner broke a tie with 6:44 left and Canada beat the United States 3-1 on Saturday night to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase for the third straight time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis left in the third quarter with a lower-body injury and didn't return in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks on Saturday after the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big Game Bound: Breaking down the matchups headed into Super Bowl weekend
item.description: The Chiefs and Eagles will kickoff Super Bowl LIX in two days from the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans, both with hopes of being crowned the kings of the NFL.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: SDCC coach battles cancer while coaching
item.description: Jose Martinez's whole life is basketball, from playing on the Puerto Rican national team at 15 years old, to becoming an assistant coach at San Diego City College.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Ohtani's former interpreter receives nearly 5-year sentence for stealing from star athlete
item.description: Ippei Mizuhara, who became a national figure for his on-field service and off-field friendship to Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani, has been sentenced to 57 months in prison for stealing millions from the Japanese phenom.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: NCAA changes participation policy on transgender athletes
item.description: The NCAA has changed its participation policy for athletes, keeping transgender students from women's sports
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Warriors to trade for Miami's Jimmy Butler: ESPN
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors are finalizing a blockbuster trade for Miami Heat star Jimmy Butler, ESPN reported Wednesday. Butler, 35, also agreed to an extension with the Warriors that will keep him with the team through the 2026-27 season, ESPN said.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Rams headed Down Under for NFL's 1st game in Australia
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) — The Los Angeles Rams are set to make NFL history Down Under next year. The squad announced Wednesday that they're headed to Melbourne, Australia, in 2026 to play the league's first regular season game in the country.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5
